mapping.a_element: 2D Bose fluid exhibiting superfluidity despite absence of true long-range order (Bose-Einstein condensation)
mapping.b_element: 2D photon fluid exhibiting Bogoliubov dispersion and sound waves as signatures of superfluidity

mapping.a_element: thermal and quantum fluctuations destroying long-range order in 2D
mapping.b_element: photon-photon collisions mediated by atoms leading to effective interactions enabling superfluid-like behavior

mapping.a_element: quasi-long-range order in finite-size 2D Bose systems
mapping.b_element: Bogoliubov dispersion relation for low-lying excitations in the photon fluid

mapping.a_element: interplay between superfluidity and condensation in finite-size systems
mapping.b_element: Landau critical-velocity criterion as a test for superfluidity in the photon fluid

mapping.a_element: Bose-Einstein condensation in interacting 2D Bose systems
mapping.b_element: Bose-Einstein condensation of photons in the nonlinear cavity

brief_justification: Both papers investigate superfluidity in 2D Bose systems, where the underlying mechanism is the emergence of low-lying collective excitations described by a Bogoliubov-like dispersion relation. The role of thermal/quantum fluctuations in destroying long-range order in 2D Bose fluids (A) corresponds to the effective photon-photon interactions enabling superfluid-like behavior in the photon fluid (B). The shared machinery is the physics of interacting 2D Bose gases, including quasi-long-range order and superfluidity criteria.
